name: Return to Tibidabo from Sant Just Desvern – Collserola Natural Park
name_original: Vuelta al Tibidabo desde Sant Just Desvern – Parque Natural de Collserola
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sant Just Desvern offer access to the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, a mountainous forest area characterized by Mediterranean vegetation, including pines and oaks. The terrain features significant elevation changes, providing challenging climbs and rewarding descents. Cyclists can experience a mix of paved paths and routes with varied gradients, suitable for different fitness levels. The region's landscape includes serene ponds, marshes, and meadows, with natural viewpoints offering expansive views over the surrounding areas. Best road cycling routes around Sant Just Desvern The most popular road cycling route is From the Turó Viewpoint to Tibidabo — Collserola Natural Park, a 24.4 miles (39.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain within the Collserola Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Return to Tibidabo from Sant Just Desvern – Collserola Natural Park, a moderate 21.7 miles (34.9 km) path. This route also explores the varied terrain of the Collserola Natural Park, including climbs towards Tibidabo. Local road cyclists also love the Gavà - Begues Climb – Church of Olesa de Bonesvalls loop from Sant Joan Despí, a 59.8 miles (96.3 km) trail leading through the wider region, often completed in about 4 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Just Desvern is defined by mountainous forest areas, Mediterranean vegetation, and panoramic viewpoints like Penya del Moro and Puig d'Olorda. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Sant Just Desvern are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 54,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Just Desvern's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Sant Just Desvern?
A: Road cycling routes around Sant Just Desvern primarily offer access to the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, a mountainous forest area. You can expect significant elevation changes, providing both challenging climbs and rewarding descents. The landscape features Mediterranean vegetation, including pines and oaks, along with serene ponds, marshes, and meadows. While many paths are paved, some routes within the park can be demanding.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Sant Just Desvern and the surrounding Collserola Natural Park offer numerous challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1028089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavà - Begues Climb – Church of Olesa de Bonesvalls loop from Sant Joan Despí&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 96.3 km trail with significant elevation gain. Other demanding options include the Tibidabo from Sant Cugat and the Santa Creu d'Olorda Hermitage – Cruz del Puig d'Olorda loop, which features the Puig d'Olorda summit.

Q: Are there easier road cycling options for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed ride?
A: While many routes involve significant climbs, there are easier options available. The region offers 28 easy road cycling tours. The Llobregat Delta – Can Camins Beach loop is noted as a more moderate, mostly paved option, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Sant Just Desvern?
A: The routes offer impressive views from natural vantage points like Penya del Moro, overlooking the Baix Llobregat region and Barcelona. The Puig d'Olorda, at 450 meters, is crowned by a fire watch tower and a large cross. You might also encounter serene ponds, marshes, and Mediterranean forests. For specific attractions, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2765506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Grotto in Parc de Torreblanca&lt;/a&gt; or exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves accessible near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area around Sant Just Desvern features several natural attractions. You can find waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. For caves, highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Juanita Mine&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt;, offering interesting detours from your ride.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Sant Just Desvern or near the Collserola Natural Park?
A: Sant Just Desvern and its proximity to the Collserola Natural Park generally offer various parking options. While specific parking lots for cyclists are not detailed, you can typically find parking in the municipality or at access points to the park, especially near popular trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Can I use public transport to access cycling routes with my bike?
A: Accessing the Collserola Natural Park and its cycling routes via public transport with a bike is often possible, though specific rules vary by transport type and time. Barcelona's public transport network, including FGC trains and some bus lines, allows bikes during off-peak hours or with specific conditions. It's recommended to check the latest regulations of the local transport providers before planning your trip.

Q: Are permits required for cycling in the Collserola Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for recreational cycling on designated paths within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. However, cyclists must adhere to park regulations, which include a 20 km/h speed limit on trails to ensure safety and preserve the natural environment. Always stay on marked paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Just Desvern?
A: May and July are particularly popular months for cyclists in Sant Just Desvern, suggesting favorable weather conditions. The Mediterranean climate generally offers pleasant cycling weather for much of the year, but spring and autumn provide milder temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides and exploring the varied terrain without extreme heat.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Sant Just Desvern?
A: The road cycling routes around Sant Just Desvern are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 800 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the rewarding descents within the mountainous forest areas of the Collserola Natural Park. The panoramic viewpoints, like Penya del Moro and Puig d'Olorda, are also frequently highlighted for their expansive views.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Just Desvern?
A: There are over 160 road cycling routes available around Sant Just Desvern, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 28 easy routes, 94 moderate routes, and 47 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.
tips: a road -formerly a forest track- recommended to go up but bad to go down, because the cement on the ground is not level and you miss the fillings with the road bike.

A large esplanade between Turó d'en Corts and Turó de l'Espinagosa, which was formed by successive dumping of rubble. In 1990 it was conditioned as a viewpoint. It has benches and different stone pines. It has 45 parking spaces. It is a good starting point for itineraries of Sant Pere Mártir.
Alternative route to avoid traffic, watch out for a hole.
A nice little cafe in La Floresta. Here you can find coffee, sandwhiches, empanadas, salads, and soups. It has options for vegetarians and vegans.
Picnic area with bar and terrace next to the old hermitage of Santa Creu d'Olorda'. Good place to rest and regain strength.
